metadata.title: Wan API
metadata.description: Test and integrate Wan API for text-to-video, image-to-video, video editing, and reference-driven AI video generation.

title: Wan API
description: Wan API brings multiple AI video workflows into one production path, including text-to-video, image-to-video, video-to-video, video editing, animation, and reference-to-video generation.
Use Wan 2.7 for high-quality 720p and 1080p generation, then switch between text, image, video, and reference modes based on the exact input your product or campaign needs.
pricingNote: USD estimates use $0.005/credit with the best annual plan. Final billing follows the selected Wan model, resolution, duration, and input type.

labels.apiDocsDetailDescription: Open the full API documentation for endpoints, authentication, task polling, and payload details.
labels.apiInputDescription: Use model-specific payloads for prompt, source media, resolution, aspect ratio, duration, callback, and generation options.
labels.selectedModelNote: The playground, request sample, request fields, and pricing on this page follow the selected model type.

featuresTitle: What Wan API adds to your video workflow
featuresDescription: The page follows the same API-first structure as the reference: test a Wan model, understand the fields, then move into integration.

capabilities.title: Text-to-video generation
capabilities.description: Turn prompts into cinematic clips with controllable resolution, aspect ratio, duration, negative prompt, seed, and prompt extension options.

capabilities.title: Image-to-video motion
capabilities.description: Animate still images into short videos while preserving subject context, composition, and visual style.

capabilities.title: Video editing workflows
capabilities.description: Use source clips and reference images to change outfits, objects, style, or motion while keeping the surrounding scene coherent.

capabilities.title: Reference-to-video control
capabilities.description: Combine reference images, videos, first frames, and voice inputs to guide characters, motion, timing, and scene layout.

quickStartTitle: From Wan playground test to production request
quickStartDescription: A compact path for teams that want to validate Wan output before connecting generation to an app or content pipeline.

workflows.title: 1. Choose a Wan mode
workflows.description: Select text-to-video, image-to-video, video edit, reference-to-video, or an older Wan workflow that matches your source input.

workflows.title: 2. Submit generation
workflows.description: Call the API with your account key, model ID, prompt, media URLs, resolution, duration, aspect ratio, and callback settings.

workflows.title: 3. Poll and use
workflows.description: Track the task until it succeeds, then retrieve the generated video URL for your app, campaign, or automation.

useCasesTitle: What you can create with Wan API
useCasesDescription: Use the API when your product needs repeatable AI video output across several input styles, not just a one-off generation.
useCases: Prompt-based social clips and short ads
Product image animation and hero videos
Video edits for outfits, props, and visual style
Batch creative generation for content pipelines

communityShowcase.title: Successful Wan generations from the community
communityShowcase.description: Browse public Wan outputs that finished successfully, then open any case to inspect the prompt, settings, and result.

productionTitle: Built for real Wan API workflows

productionNotes.title: Account-gated execution
productionNotes.body: Users sign in, submit jobs, and consume credits through the existing SdanceAI generation flow.

productionNotes.title: Flexible source media
productionNotes.body: Wan workflows can accept prompt-only requests or media-driven requests with images, videos, first frames, references, and audio where supported.

productionNotes.title: Resolution-aware cost
productionNotes.body: Choose 720p for iteration and 1080p when a selected concept is ready for a sharper final render.

faqs.question: What is Wan API best for?
faqs.answer: It is best for teams that need several AI video workflows in one integration, including text-to-video, image-to-video, video editing, video-to-video, animation, and reference-guided generation.

faqs.question: Which Wan models are available on this page?
faqs.answer: The page follows the configured Wan family, including Wan 2.7 text-to-video, image-to-video, video edit, reference-to-video, Wan 2.6 workflows, Wan 2.5 workflows, and Wan 2.2 animation modes.

faqs.question: What is the difference between text-to-video and image-to-video?
faqs.answer: Text-to-video starts from a prompt only. Image-to-video uses a source image so the generated clip can preserve the subject, framing, or visual style from the input.

faqs.question: When should I use Wan 2.7 Reference to Video?
faqs.answer: Use Reference to Video when you need tighter control from reference images, reference videos, first frames, or voice input rather than relying only on a written prompt.

faqs.question: Does Wan API support 1080p output?
faqs.answer: Wan 2.7 entries on this page include 720p and 1080p pricing rows. Choose the resolution that matches your quality and cost target.

faqs.question: Can I edit an existing video?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use Wan 2.7 Video Edit or another video-input workflow when you want to modify a source clip with prompts and references.

faqs.question: Can I use negative prompts?
faqs.answer: Supported Wan request schemas include negative prompt fields for reducing blur, distortion, low quality output, and other unwanted details.

faqs.question: Where can I test Wan before integrating?
faqs.answer: Use the playground on this page to test the model, prompt, media URLs, resolution, duration, and API payload behavior before moving into production.

faqs.question: How should I manage cost?
faqs.answer: Start with lower resolution and shorter clips for prompt exploration, then switch to 1080p or longer durations only after the direction is validated.

faqs.question: Can I use Wan API for commercial projects?
faqs.answer: This page is configured for production video workflows. For commercial use, review your account plan, credit balance, model availability, and applicable content policy requirements.

cta.title: Turn Wan into your video workflow
cta.description: Open the generator, test a Wan prompt or reference workflow, then use the API docs when you are ready to connect Wan to production.
